title: Death toll rises to two after minibus overturns in Idukki, 22 injured
content: Two people were killed and 22 others were injured after a minibus carrying tourists overturned at Anayirankal in Pooppara here on Thursday. The deceased are Stella Mary (58) and Bhagya Jyothi, both natives of Virudhunagar in Tamil Nadu. The accident occurred around 4 pm. The 28-member tourist group- consisting of 18 women, four men and six children- from Virudhunagar and Aruppukottai was returning after visiting Munnar. According to reports, the driver lost control of the vehicle while taking a curve. The bus crashed through a culvert and plunged more than 50 feet into a tea plantation below. Local residents and passengers from other vehicles joined the rescue efforts and pulled out those trapped inside the bus. Rescue workers said Stella Mary had sustained serious head injuries. She was rushed to Tata Highrange Hospital in Munnar but succumbed to her injuries. The other injured passengers are undergoing treatment at the hospital, while six seriously injured persons were shifted to Theni Medical College Hospital for advanced treatment.
